=== Metadata ===
Effect version: 3.19.19

/.src/globalErrorInEffectCatch.ts(14,36): warning TS377022: The `catch` callback in `Effect.tryPromise` returns the global `Error` type. Untagged errors merge together in the Effect error channel and lose type-level distinction; a tagged error preserves that distinction and can wrap the original error in a `cause` property. effect(globalErrorInEffectCatch)
/.src/globalErrorInEffectCatch.ts(16,16): warning TS377023: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)
/.src/globalErrorInEffectCatch.ts(24,61): warning TS377022: The `catch` callback in `Effect.tryMapPromise` returns the global `Error` type. Untagged errors merge together in the Effect error channel and lose type-level distinction; a tagged error preserves that distinction and can wrap the original error in a `cause` property. effect(globalErrorInEffectCatch)
/.src/globalErrorInEffectCatch.ts(26,21): warning TS377023: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)


==== /.src/globalErrorInEffectCatch.ts (4 errors) ====
    // @effect-v3
    import { Data, Effect } from "effect"
    import * as Function from "effect/Function"
    
    class MyError extends Data.TaggedError("MyError")<{
      cause: unknown
    }> {}
    
    export const shouldNotTrigger = Effect.tryPromise({
      try: () => fetch("http://something"),
      catch: (e) => new MyError({ cause: e })
    })
    
    export const shouldTriggerAsWell = Effect.tryPromise({
                                       ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377022: The `catch` callback in `Effect.tryPromise` returns the global `Error` type. Untagged errors merge together in the Effect error channel and lose type-level distinction; a tagged error preserves that distinction and can wrap the original error in a `cause` property. effect(globalErrorInEffectCatch)
      try: () => fetch("http://something"),
      catch: () => new Error("Unknown!")
                   ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377023: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)
    })
    
    export const shouldNotTrigger2 = Effect.succeed(42).pipe(Effect.tryMapPromise({
      try: (_) => fetch("http://something/" + _),
      catch: (e) => new MyError({ cause: e })
    }))
    
    export const shouldTriggerAsWell2 = Effect.succeed(42).pipe(Effect.tryMapPromise({
                                                                ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377022: The `catch` callback in `Effect.tryMapPromise` returns the global `Error` type. Untagged errors merge together in the Effect error channel and lose type-level distinction; a tagged error preserves that distinction and can wrap the original error in a `cause` property. effect(globalErrorInEffectCatch)
      try: (_) => fetch("http://something/" + _),
      catch: (cause) => new Error("Hey", { cause })
                        ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377023: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)
    }))
